title: Walking Skeleton, Core Domain, and Cumulative Construction · Product Reasoning Brief: Quality Floor
summary: The capstone has extensive infrastructure and design artifacts but no small deployed path proving that user value and all critical engineering gates can coexist. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone quality floor increment in deployed cumulative capstone core. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Keep accessibility, security, privacy, tests, observability, documentation, deployability, and rollback at an explicit floor in every revision.
For each increment, retrieve prior requirements, predict behavior, implement, test accepted and rejected cases, validate with users, and update claims.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Quality Floor · Theory Quality Floor
steps.instruction:
theory case — Infrastructure and design artifacts grow while no deployed end-to-end slice proves user value can coexist with the full quality floor. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Quality Floor contract for the supplied data.

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for keep accessibility, security, privacy, tests, observability, documentation, deployability, and rollback at an explicit floor in every revision. content.text: Infrastructure and design artifacts grow while no deployed end-to-end slice proves user value can coexist with the full quality floor. The theory variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Quality Floor while producing deployed cumulative capstone core.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Infrastructure and design artifacts grow while no deployed end-to-end slice proves user value can coexist with the full quality floor. The worked example variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Keep accessibility, security, privacy, tests, observability, documentation, deployability, and rollback at an explicit floor in every revision. A defensible solution applies Quality Floor, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to deployed cumulative capstone core.

content.code:
# Worked focus: capstone-quality-floor — Keep accessibility, security, privacy, tests, observability, documentation, deployability, and rollback at an explicit floor in every revision.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-4
focus: capstone-quality-floor
decision-evidence: Keep accessibility, security, privacy, tests, observability, documentation, deployability, and rollback at an explicit floor in every revision.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Quality can be accumulated in a final hardening phase. This fails the Quality Floor contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Research anchor
content.value: NIST Privacy Framework (Privacy Framework 1.0 final; 1.1 remains an initial public draft at review) — Controls purpose, data processing, governance, communication, protection, retention, correction, and privacy-risk reasoning without presenting the draft as final.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep accessibility, security, privacy, tests, observability, documentation, deployability, and rollback at an explicit floor in every revision.

steps.title: Defend the Quality Floor model · Theory Quality Floor
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Quality Floor: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Infrastructure and design artifacts grow while no deployed end-to-end slice proves user value can coexist with the full quality floor. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Evidence target: Defend the Quality Floor model · Theory Quality Floor.

For Defend the Quality Floor model · Theory Quality Floor, keep Quality Floor connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Keep accessibility, security, privacy, tests, observability, documentation, deployability, and rollback at an explicit floor in every revision.
